Fntn Porrrtcel PnacrrcEs CoivrrvussroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5ffi o Fax (916) 322-A886
October 22,2W
AshPirayou
Pirayou Law Offices i
695O Almaden Expressway, Suite 125
San Jose, CA95l2A
Your Request forAdvice
Our File No. A-A+221
DearMr. Pirayou:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf the Comrrittee to protect
Neighborhood Libraries - Yes on Measure S, and the San Jose Taxpa.yers for a Secure
Airport - Yes on Measrne A corrmittees for advice regarding the use Lf n*4" provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the'lA,ct')-l Please notc, thc Cornmission will not adrrise
with respect to Past conduct. (Regulation 18329OX8XA).) Therefore, mthing in this
letter should be eonstued to evaluate any corduct *tri"n rnuy have already taken placg
and any conclusions containd hcrein apply onlytoprospective actions.
QUESTTON
May the Measure A Committeg a local ballot m@sure committee, contribute a
portion or all of its current cash on hand to the Measure SCommittee, a local balloi
measure committee, to support thepassage of Measrne S without violating the.frersonal
use" restrictions of the Act?
CONCLUSION
The Measure A Comrrittee may contribute aportion or all of its current cash on
hand to the Measrne S Committee to support thepassage of Measrre S without violating
the "personal use" reshictions of the Act so long as the-transfer does not have a
substantial personal benefit on any person who directs or controls the activities of the
Measure A Committee-
I Government Code sections 8 I 000 - 91014- Conrmission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
Re:


File No. A-04-221
Page No. 2
trACTS
You serve as the treasurer and legal counsel to the above-mentioned committees
and are authorized bythe committees to seek a formal opinion on theirbehalf. The San
Jose Taxpayers for a Secure Ailport - Yes on Measure A ("Measure A Committee') was
formed on December 12,2N2, as a primarily formed committee to support the passage
of Measure A, dealing wittr airport security and traffic relief. When oompteting the Form
4lQ the Measure A Committee did not state any disposition of srrplus fimds, as such an
option was not available on the qrrrent Fonn 410. The Measure A Committee
campaign's voter outreach efforts to San Jose yoters emphasized the importance of
making the City of San Jose a better city urith a world-class airport- The Measure A
Committee was a local committee filing its disclosure reports with the San Jose city clerk
and was comprised of individuals represenrting the local labor and business communities.
The Measure A Committee has retained you to provide professional heasurer and legal
services to ensure cornpliance with the Political Reform Act, and has retained Dustin
DeRollo (via his association with Platinum Advisors) as a committee cmrpaign
consultant. The Measure A Committeehas also retained Staton & Hughes as a
committee campaign consultant. Neith€r Mr. DeRollo, Staton & Hughes nor you have
any authority to approve the expeirditure of the committee's funds, absent the express
approval of the Measure A Committee members. No vendors participated as members of
the committee.
Measure A was approved bythe voters of San Jose in March 2003. Following
approval ofthe Measure A and thepayment of all outstanding invoices, the Measure A
Committee had a balance of $57,660 in campaign funds.
The Committee to Protect Neighborhood Libraries -Yes on Measure S
("Measure S committee') was formed on August 6,za0/,, as a primarily formed
committee to pass Measure S, a continuation of an annual parcel tax funding City of San
Jose library operations. Measure S Committee members are nearly identical to the
members of the Measure A Committee, including representatives ofthe labor and
business communities. The Measure S Committee is a local committee filing its
disclosure reports with the San Jose city clerk. The Measure S Committee has also
retaind you to provide professional treasurer and legal serrrices to ensure compliance
with the Act, as well as Mr. DeRollo (via his association with Saggau & DeRoilo ,LLC)
as a committee campaign consultant. Staton & Hugfues are also raai""a as a oommittee
campaign consultant. Neither Mr. DeRollo, Staton & Hugles nor you have any authorit5r
to approve the expenditme of the committee's fimds, auserrt the express app,roval of the
Measure S Committe merrbers. No vendorsparticipated as mernbers ofthe oommittee.
The Measr-rre S voter outreach efforts me inteirded to make voters of San Jose, on
November 2,2OO4, support the passage of Measure S in order to continue making San
Jose a better city and one of the nation's most livable communities by ensuring ongoing
library senrices to residents.


File No. A-04-221
Page No. 3
ANALYSIS
The Act contains specific provisions governing the use of campaign funds.
(Sections 895 I 0-89522.) T\ese sections are generally referred to as the 'f)ersonal use"
provisions. Most of these provisions me applicable to both candidates and committees,
including statewide ballot measure committees. (Section 8951l.) However, the personal
use statutes do not contain a specific provision relating to the perrnissible disposition of
funds held by a ballot me:rsure committee after the election. The onlyp,rovision which is
specifically directed to leftover or "surplus" funds is section 895l9,and this section
pertains to the disposition of funds held by candidates rather than by ballot measure
committees. (See, Hiltachk Advice Letter, No. I-90-053.) We have therefore advised
that a ballot measure committee may use its firnds for anypurpose permitted by sections
89510 - 89518.
Generally, this permits the expenditure of carrpaip fimds provided that the
expenditrne is reasonably relatd to a political, legislative or governmental purpose.
However, the expenditure must be directly related to a political, govemmental or
legislative purpose if it will confer a zubstantial personal b€nefit upon any person who
directs or controls the activities of the committee or if it is the tlpe of expenditure for
which the personal use provisions specifically require a direct relationship. (Spatn
Advice Letter, No. I-90-410; see, e.g., section s9512.) Regulation 18960 defines a
"direct personal benefit" as follows:
"(a) Forpurposes of Government Code srction
8951 1OX3), an expenditure of carnpaip fimds results in a
direct personal benefit when, u/ithin six months of the
expenditure and without the assistance of any interverring
influence or internrption, the candidate or electd officer or
member of his or her immediate family
'(1) Realizes an increase in his or her income or assets, or a
decrease in his orher expenses orliabilities, ofmore than
$200 from the expenditure; or
*(2) Actually makes personal use of an asset obtained as a
result of the exper:rditure.
*(b) An expenditrne of campaign funds does not result in a
prohibited direct personal benefit if othernrise spocifically
permitted under any other provisions of Article 4
(commencing with Section 89510) of Chapter 9.5 of Title 9
of the Governrnent Code, or interpretative regulations
thereto-"
According to your facts, while the two committees have some sharod
consultants that may benefit from the transfer of the funds in question, none of the
shared consultants direct or control the activities ofthe committee. Therefore, so


FileNo. A-04-221
page No. 4
long as the transfer does not have a substantial personal benefit on anyperson
who directs or controls the activities of the committee, the transfer would be
permissible under the Act.
If you have any other questions regmding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Galena West
Counsel, I.egal Division
Gwje
